name: Crockpot Beef Roll-Ups
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the beef round into strips that are 1/2 inch thick.
2. Trim any excess fat and remove the bone from the beef.
3. Pound the beef strips with a meat mallet or on a saucer until they are an even thickness.
4. Slice the bacon into strips that are about 1 inch wide and 4 inches long.
5. Place bacon slices on top of the beef strips and roll them up, securing each one with small wooden picks.
6. Brown the rolled-up meat in hot oil in a large frying pan.
7. Remove the rolls from the frying pan and transfer them to the crockpot.
8. In the frying pan, add the onion soup mix and 2 cups of water. Simmer for 3 minutes, scraping any particles from the bottom of the pan.
9. Pour the liquid mixture over the rolls in the crock pot.
10. Cook until the meat is tender.
11. Remove the cooked rolls to a warm serving dish.
12. In a small bowl, combine the cornstarch and 1/2 cup water until smooth.
13. Add this mixture to the remaining liquid from the crockpot and simmer, stirring constantly, for 3-4 minutes or until thickened.
14. Pour the gravy over the rolls and garnish with parsley and cherry tomatoes.
